title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Contract Changed Case Tests
summary: A client library release needs RoundTripper fakes, httptest servers, TLS fixtures, truncation faults, contract tests, and low-cardinality telemetry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go contract changed case tests increment in a Go 1.26 net/http fault-injected client verification harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
